text: The surgeon uses an electrocautery and harmonic scalpel to dissect through dense adhesions and scar tissue surrounding the gastric band, preparing to access the stomach for band removal.

The surgeon locates the buckle of the gastric band embedded within the stomach wall, noting its dark brown color due to exposure to stomach acid. The surgeon prepares to cut the buckle loose for removal, while introducing a suction device to manage expected stomach contents.
The surgeon stitches up the large hole left in the stomach wall after the removal of the gastric band, using several sutures to close the defect.

The surgeon has closed the hole in the stomach with sutures. The contaminated gastric band is placed inside a sterile bag to prevent contact with the abdominal wall and incision, and is removed from the abdominal cavity. A drain is placed as a precautionary measure.
Thank you so much for watching and be sure to subscribe for future videos.
